codeReview.summary: The fix removes the flaky hash-overlap check, but the replacement assertion no longer validates the intended minimal-write invariant. The updated test can pass even when all chunks are rewritten, so correctness coverage is weakened.

issues.description: `XCTAssertLessThanOrEqual(finalChunkCount, initialChunkCount * 2)` is not a minimal-write assertion. A full rewrite with similar chunk cardinality still passes this bound, so the test no longer verifies that only touched chunks are rewritten. This violates the feature requirement to keep incremental-write correctness coverage while fixing determinism.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The worker transcript does not show the required reads of `.factory/library/architecture.md` and `.factory/library/user-testing.md`, even though `indexing-efficiency-worker/SKILL.md` mandates them in Step 1. The handoff still reports `skillFeedback.followedProcedure: true`, which appears inconsistent with the transcript skeleton.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Transcript skeleton for worker session `ae347eda-b99b-4262-8757-e59fb725ae31` shows reads for mission files and AGENTS/test sources, but no reads for `.factory/library/architecture.md` or `.factory/library/user-testing.md`; skill requirement in `.factory/skills/indexing-efficiency-worker/SKILL.md` Step 1 explicitly requires both files.

summary: Reviewed handoff, transcript skeleton, skill contract, and commit `9f97571`. The determinism fix passes tests but weakens the target contract: current assertions do not prove minimal writes, so this feature should be considered failed for correctness coverage.
